Class Transformer

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractOperator
org.key_project.logic.op.AbstractSortedOperator
org.key_project.logic.op.Function
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.JFunction
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.Transformer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Sorted, Named, Operator, SortedOperator, Sorted, SyntaxElement
public class Transformer extends JFunction
Functions with a restricted/special rule set only applicable for the top level of the term transformer and not directly for its arguments, prohibiting any rule applications to sub arguments as well as applications from outside such as update applications. They work similar to the idea of 'Predicate Transformer Semantics' as introduced by Dijkstra in "Guarded commands, nondeterminacy and formal derivation of programs". Note that in the taclets, arguments such as "a -> b" need to be written as "(a -> b)", in order to let the parser know that the argument is "a -> b" and not "a" followed by a syntactic error.

  • Constructor Details

    • Transformer

      public Transformer(Name name, Sort sort, ImmutableArray<Sort> argSorts)

    • Transformer

      public Transformer(Name name, Sort argSort)

  • Method Details

    • getTransformer

      public static Transformer getTransformer(Name name, Sort sort, ImmutableArray<Sort> argSorts, TermServices services)
      Looks up the function namespace for a term transformer with the given attributes, assuming it to be uniquely defined by its name. If none is found, a new term transformer is created.
      Parameters:
      name - name of the term transformer
      sort - sort of the term transformer
      argSorts - array of the transformer's argument sorts
      services -
      Returns:
      the term transformer of interest

    • getTransformer

      public static Transformer getTransformer(Transformer t, TermServices services)
      Takes a template for a term transformer and checks in the function namespace, whether an equivalent already exists. If this is the case, it returns the found transformer, otherwise it creates a new one.
      Parameters:
      t - the template for a term transformer
      services -
      Returns:
      the term transformer to be used

    • inTransformer

      public static boolean inTransformer(PosInOccurrence pio)
      Examines a position for whether it is inside a term transformer.
      Parameters:
      pio - A position in an occurrence of a term
      Returns:
      true if inside a term transformer, false otherwise

    • getTransformer

      public static Transformer getTransformer(PosInOccurrence pio)
      Examines a position for whether it is inside a term transformer. If this is the case, the found term transformer is returned.
      Parameters:
      pio - A position in an occurrence of a term
      Returns:
      the term transformer the position is in, null otherwise
